libraryDescription:
This library, gui.CheckBoxWindow, provides a window containing arranged check boxes as a GUI component.

In the standard VCSSL library GUI, GUI components are handled as int IDs, but the GUI component provided by this module is handled as theCheckBoxWindow struct.

The CheckBoxWindow struct internally stores resource IDs and related data, but you normally do not need to be aware of its internals becausefunctions for creating and destroying it are provided. Direct access to its members is not recommended.
Specifically, create it with the newCheckBoxWindow function and destroy it with thedeleteComponent function.

This module also provides overloads of standard GUI library functions so that the CheckBoxWindow struct can be handled almost in the sameway as in the standard library GUI.
For example, the mountComponent, setComponentSize, andsetComponentLocation functions are provided.

function0.name: newCheckBoxWindow
function0.declaration: CheckBoxWindow newCheckBoxWindow(int x, int y, int width, int height, string title, string labelText[ ], bool[ ] defaultValue)
function0.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the window. 
(int type) width : The width of the window. 
(int type) height : The height of the window. 
(string type) title : The title of the window. 
(string[] type) labelText : The array storing the label text of all check boxes. 
defaultText : The array storing the default values of all check boxes.
function0.returnValue: (CheckBoxWindow type) The created check box window.

function1.name: newCheckBoxWindow
function1.description: Creates a check box window with its size determined automatically.
function1.parameters:
(string[] type) labelText : The array storing the label text of all check boxes. 
defaultText : The array storing the default values of all check boxes.
function1.returnValue: (CheckBoxWindow type) The created check box window.

function2.name: deleteComponent
function2.parameters: (CheckBoxWindow type) windowInstance : The window to be destroyed.

function3.name: setButton
function3.description: Displays a button at the bottom of the window.
function3.parameters:
(CheckBoxWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(string type) label : The label text of the button.

function4.name: getButton
function4.description: Gets the component ID of the button.
function4.returnValue: (int type) The component ID of the button.

function5.name: getWindow
function5.description: Gets the GUI component ID of the window.
function5.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the window.

function6.name: getCheckBox
function6.description: Gets the text field corresponding to the specified label.
function6.parameters:
(CheckBoxWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(string type) labelText : The label text corresponding to the check box to get.
function6.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the check box.

function7.name: getCheckBox
function7.description: Gets the text field at the specified index.
function7.parameters:
(CheckBoxWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(int type) index : The index of the check box.
function7.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the check box.

function8.name: getComponentSize
function8.description: Returns the size of the window.
function8.returnValue: (int[] type) The array storing the size of the window.

function9.name: setComponentSize
function9.declaration: void setComponentSize(CheckBoxWindow &windowInstance, int width, int height)
function9.description: Sets the size of the window.
function9.parameters:
(CheckBoxWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) width : The height.
function9.returnValue: (void type) The array storing the size of the window.

function10.name: setComponentSize
function10.description: Automatically adjusts the sizes of the internal components to match the current window size. Use this when the window size has been changed externally.

function11.name: getComponentLocation
function11.description: Returns the location of the window.
function11.returnValue: (int[] type) The array storing the location of the window.

function12.name: setComponentLocation
function12.declaration: void setComponentLocation(CheckBoxWindow &windowInstance, int x, int y)
function12.description: Sets the location of the window.
function12.parameters:
(CheckBoxWindow type) windowInstance : The target window. 
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the window.
